Allow analyzing Raw $2 Ore from base stones.

extras.tinactory.technology.alloy_smelting.deta: A process that combines metals under high temperatures, producing specialized alloys with enhanced properties for advanced construction and manufacturing applications.
extras.tinactory.technology.steel.details: A versatile and durable construction material, essential for building strong structures and crafting advanced tools and equipment.
extras.tinactory.technology.motor.details: High-powered mechanical devices that drive machinery and vehicles, enabling dynamic movement and automation in various operations.
extras.tinactory.technology.pump_and_piston.det: Hydraulic systems designed to transfer fluids and generate force, crucial for powering heavy machinery and regulating industrial processes.
extras.tinactory.technology.electric_heating.de: A reliable heating solution that uses electricity to maintain optimal temperatures in industrial processes, ensuring consistent quality and energy efficiency.
extras.tinactory.technology.material_cutting.de: Precision technology for slicing and shaping raw materials into desired forms, enhancing manufacturing speed and allowing for complex designs.
extras.tinactory.technology.conveyor_module.det: An automated transport system designed to move resources efficiently between production lines, streamlining workflow and boosting productivity in your facilities.
extras.tinactory.technology.hot_working.details: A metal-forming technique that involves shaping materials at high temperatures, increasing their ductility and strength while allowing for complex designs and structural refinements.
extras.tinactory.technology.battery.details: A compact energy storage unit that provides a reliable power source for machinery, electronics, and backup systems, essential for maintaining continuous operation and energy efficiency.
A precision pairing where sensors detect environmental changes or objects, while emitters send out signals or energy, enabling automated control, tracking, and safety monitoring across complex systems.
A compact, multi-functional chip that combines numerous electronic components, streamlining processing power and control for advanced machinery and automation systems.
extras.tinactory.technology.robot_arm.details: A versatile, programmable mechanical arm designed for precise movements, ideal for tasks such as assembly, welding, and material handling in high-efficiency production lines.
extras.tinactory.technology.kanthal.details: A high-resistance metal alloy known for its durability and ability to withstand extreme temperatures, commonly used in heating elements and industrial furnaces for efficient, long-lasting performance.
extras.tinactory.technology.sifting.details: A filtration process that separates materials based on particle size, essential for refining raw resources and ensuring quality in production by isolating unwanted elements.
extras.tinactory.technology.autofarm.details: A fully automated agricultural system that plants, harvests, and processes crops with minimal human input, maximizing food production and resource efficiency across your settlement.
extras.tinactory.technology.chemistry.details: The scientific foundation for creating new materials and compounds, enabling advanced manufacturing, fuel production, and the development of powerful industrial processes.
extras.tinactory.technology.cleanroom.details: A controlled environment with minimal contaminants, essential for producing high-precision components like microchips and pharmaceuticals where even microscopic particles can compromise quality.
extras.tinactory.technology.cold_working.detail: A metal-forming process performed at room temperature, increasing material strength through deformation while maintaining precision and a smooth surface finish.
extras.tinactory.technology.cpu.details: The central processing unit that serves as the brain of automated systems, executing commands and managing complex operations with speed and precision.
extras.tinactory.technology.distillation.detail: A separation process that uses heat to isolate and purify liquids based on boiling points, essential for refining fuels, chemicals, and other valuable resources.
extras.tinactory.technology.electrolyzing.detai: A process that uses electric current to break down compounds into their elemental components, crucial for extracting pure materials like hydrogen, oxygen, and metals in advanced production chains.
extras.tinactory.technology.nichrome.details: A durable, high-resistance alloy commonly used in heating elements, valued for its ability to withstand high temperatures and maintain performance over prolonged use.
extras.tinactory.technology.oil_processing.deta: The refinement of crude oil into usable products like fuel, lubricants, and chemicals, forming a critical backbone for powering machinery and producing advanced materials.
extras.tinactory.technology.organic_chemistry.d: The study and manipulation of carbon-based compounds, enabling the creation of plastics, pharmaceuticals, fuels, and other essential materials in advanced manufacturing and research.
extras.tinactory.technology.pyrolyse_oven.detai: A high-temperature chamber that thermally decomposes organic materials in the absence of oxygen, used to recycle waste and produce valuable byproducts like creosote oil.
extras.tinactory.technology.soldering.details: A technique that joins metal components by melting a filler metal, creating strong electrical and mechanical connections essential for assembling electronic circuits and devices.
extras.tinactory.technology.vacuum_freezer.deta: A specialized cooling system that removes air to lower pressure, allowing materials to freeze quickly at lower temperatures and preserving delicate items without ice crystal formation.
Unlocks complex reactions that allow the synthesis of powerful compounds and rare materials. This technology is key to producing advanced fuels, polymers, and high-efficiency catalysts.
extras.tinactory.technology.arc_furnace.details: Uses high electrical currents to reach extreme temperatures, melting even the toughest ores and alloys. It drastically increases smelting speed and purity compared to traditional furnaces.
extras.tinactory.technology.autoclave.details: A high-pressure chamber that enables precise chemical and material processing. It’s essential for sterilization, advanced composites, and extracting resources otherwise locked away.
extras.tinactory.technology.digital_storage.det: Replaces bulky warehouses with compact, high-capacity memory banks. This allows for instant data access, organization, and control of large production networks.
extras.tinactory.technology.hydrometallurgy.det: Extracts metals using aqueous chemistry instead of heat, making recovery of rare elements more efficient. It’s particularly useful for reclaiming valuable materials from low-grade ores.
extras.tinactory.technology.lithography.details: Etches microscopic circuits onto silicon wafers to create advanced microchips. This breakthrough enables the leap into high-speed computing and automation.
extras.tinactory.technology.oil_cracking.detail: Breaks down heavy crude into lighter, more useful fuels and chemicals. This process unlocks plastics, synthetic materials, and efficient energy production.
extras.tinactory.technology.rocket_science.deta: Opens the path to space exploration with advanced propulsion and guidance systems. Mastering it is crucial for building rockets and satellites.
extras.tinactory.technology.rocket_t1.details: A basic spacecraft capable of reaching low orbit or nearby planetary bodies. It marks the first step toward interplanetary industry and colonization.
extras.tinactory.technology.tnt.details: A powerful explosive useful for mining, demolition, and warfare. Its destructive force makes it both a valuable tool and a dangerous weapon.
extras.tinactory.technology.surface_mount_devic: A breakthrough in electronics that allows components to be mounted directly onto circuit boards. This miniaturization leads to faster, more compact, and more reliable devices.
extras.tinactory.technology.tungsten_steel.deta: A super-hard alloy that resists heat and wear far better than ordinary steel. It’s indispensable for high-performance tools, armor, and cutting-edge machines.
extras.tinactory.technology.metal_former.detail: A precision machine that bends, presses, and shapes metals into complex parts. It enables mass production of durable components for advanced engineering.
extras.tinactory.technology.large_boiler.detail: A high-capacity thermal unit that converts fuel into steady industrial-grade steam. Its reinforced chambers and efficient heat-exchange design support continuous operation, powering chemical processes and energy systems with reliable pressure output.
extras.tinactory.technology.multi_smelter.detai: An industrial furnace capable of processing multiple ores simultaneously. Its efficiency saves time, energy, and space in large-scale metallurgical operations.
extras.tinactory.technology.large_turbine.detai: A heavy-duty power unit that channels high-pressure steam through multi-stage blades to generate rotational energy. Engineered for stability and throughput, it delivers strong, continuous mechanical power for industrial energy grids.
extras.tinactory.technology.advanced_polymer.de: High-strength synthetic materials designed for extreme durability and flexibility. They’re essential for advanced construction, protective gear, and high-performance machinery.
extras.tinactory.technology.carbon_fiber.detail: An ultra-light yet incredibly strong composite made from tightly bound carbon strands. It’s ideal for aerospace structures, advanced vehicles, and high-efficiency machinery where strength-to-weight ratio is critical.
extras.tinactory.technology.power_substation.de: A critical hub that distributes electricity from generators to factories and cities. It stabilizes voltage, prevents overloads, and ensures reliable energy flow across your entire network.
extras.tinactory.technology.rocket_t2.details: A powerful spacecraft equipped for interplanetary travel and heavier payloads. It expands exploration beyond nearby orbits, enabling resource transport and colony establishment on distant worlds.
extras.tinactory.technology.ender_chemistry.det: Harnesses the mysterious energies of the Ender dimension to manipulate matter in impossible ways. Its unstable reactions defy conventional science, unlocking materials and phenomena beyond the known universe.
extras.tinactory.technology.platinum_group_meta: A collection of rare, lustrous elements prized for their exceptional catalytic and conductive properties. They are vital for high-end electronics, advanced catalysts, and cutting-edge energy systems.
extras.tinactory.technology.nuclear_physics.det: Explores the forces binding atomic nuclei, unlocking immense power from the heart of matter itself. This knowledge paves the way for nuclear energy, advanced reactors, and devastating weaponry.

quests.tinactory.quests.00_ultra_low_voltage.su: Alone on a void island: few blocks underfoot, endless possibilities ahead.
quests.tinactory.quests.00_ultra_low_voltage.00: Plant Trees First
Begin Industry
Welcome to Tinactory!
No vast continent here--just you, an obsidian platform, and a starter crate. Every industrial empire begins with a few logs.

Open the starter crate, take a sapling, and plant it on the island's flat corner. When it grows, chop your first log. Wood is your first step toward machines: planks, tools, fuel, and supports all start here.
First wooden board: start of your industrial journey. From now on, the Void is no longer empty--it will be filled with your creativity and the roar of machines.
Learn to create or join a team
The road to industry is great, but building a dream alone is exhausting. Tinactory has its own team system so you can share machines, tech, and progress with partners.
In singleplayer, or if you want to play alone, press the ~ key to open the Technology Screen and enter any name to create a solo team. This binds your tech and machines to that team.
On a server, you can also use this command:
/tinactory addPlayerToTeam <player>
to invite another player to your team.
If server spawn management is enabled, these actions also create a sky island and set spawn for easier team starts.
Note: FTBQuests cannot directly use vanilla teams, so Tinactory teams do not sync quest progress automatically.
To share quest progress with teammates, create a team with the same name in FTBTeams.
Too Lonely Alone
Stone from the Void
Make a Primitive Cobblestone Generator
Wood no longer fits your industrial ambition. Time to summon sturdier cobblestone from the void.
Build a Primitive Cobblestone Generator. It needs no complex power, just some wood and wit, and it can squeeze cobblestone from the void.
Ore Begins
Analyze and wash ore
With cobblestone, explore its hidden ores. Make a Primitive Ore Analyzer and Primitive Ore Washer to begin your mining tech age.

Put cobblestone into the analyzer for a chance to find ore crystals. Do not be surprised; this is one of Tinactory's core mechanics. For now, you can only find iron and copper, but more ores will appear as your tech advances.
Raw ore from analysis cannot be used directly and must be washed. Make a Primitive Ore Washer and supply it with water to get clean ore samples.
Tip: use the starter chest's two water buckets to make infinite water and keep your washer running.
Hammering Begins
Make a Workbench and first tools
You are no longer a primitive worker using only your hands. To make industry run, learn to use tools and the Workbench.
First, make a Workbench.
It looks like an upgraded crafting table, but does more. Some recipes require specific tools and consume tool durability. Place tools in the tool slots below the Workbench to use them in crafting.
Of course, for ordinary items it can also work like a vanilla crafting table.
Next, make your first two tools:
Stone Hammer: crushes ores, a key early ore-processing tool.
Flint Mortar: grinds some materials into dust, preparing later smelting steps.
Tip:
If multiple tools are in the Workbench, some recipes may conflict and hide the result you want. Remove tools you do not need for now to craft smoothly.
Birth of Metal
Refine iron and copper
At last, the true metal age begins. Use your tools and machines to turn stone into shining metal.
The Ore Analyzer can sometimes extract Pyrite Ore and Chalcopyrite Ore from cobblestone. Raw ores cannot be used directly and need several refining steps.
First, crush the ore twice with a hammer to make fine dust. Then put the dust in the Ore Washer and wash away impurities to get iron dust and copper dust.
Finally, smelt these dusts in a furnace to get your first iron ingot and copper ingot.
This marks your entry into the metal age. The heart of machinery starts beating.
Roasting
Wrought Iron
It is time to make your first wrought iron. This key material lets you craft a full tool set and take a step toward precision machinery.
At this stage, the only way to get wrought iron is by roasting iron nuggets. The process is not simple; complete these steps patiently:
1. Grind iron ingot into dust.
2. Split dust into small piles.
3. Smelt small dust piles to nuggets.
4. Smelt nuggets into wrought nuggets.
5. Grind wrought nuggets into small dust piles.
6. Combine small wrought dust piles into dust.
7. Smelt once more to get real wrought iron.
Luckily, wrought iron is only for tools right now, so you need little. Those tools lead to higher voltage and complex machines.
Wrought Iron Tools
Craft your first metal tool set
You have wrought iron. Craft wrought iron tools.
These tools will help you process metal. You do not need to make every tool at once, but it is best to complete them over time because later machines depend on them.
Tools include: File, Hammer, Mortar, Saw, Screwdriver, Wire Cutter, and Wrench.
With these tools, you can process ores and metal parts more efficiently, building a firm base for industry.
Basic ULV Components
Make ULV Machine Hulls, cables, and Vacuum Tubes
You are about to enter the Ultra Low Voltage (ULV) stage, an important transition before electrification. Many materials still depend on your wrought iron tools here.
In Tinactory, each voltage tier needs three core component types: Machine Hulls, cables, and circuits. The materials differ by voltage, but their roles are similar.
ULV Machine Hulls and cables both use iron processing. They form the first structure of your electric machines. ULV circuits rely on Vacuum Tubes for control for now.
As tech improves, Vacuum Tubes will be replaced by better circuits. For now, they open the electric industrial age.
Steam Power
Power with steam machines
To truly run your factory, you need your own power source. Now make a Small Steam Boiler and ULV Steam Turbine to start your first power line.

Put fuel and water into the Steam Boiler, and it will heat up and make steam. The boiler has a temperature; if it gets too hot, it cannot keep making steam, so keep adding water to maintain a proper temperature.
Feed boiler steam into the ULV Steam Turbine, and it will run and output power.
Move steam two ways:
Make an Iron Fluid Cell and manually scoop and pour steam.
Use a Logistic Worker for automated steam transfer, explained in another quest.
quests.tinactory.quests.00_ultra_low_voltage.01: Assembler
Make a ULV Assembler
Now make your first key device: the ULV Assembler. This machine is central to the whole factory system. It can automate many complex crafts and is required for most components and machines.

In the ULV stage, many components and machines can still be crafted with tools in the Workbench, but this is temporary. At higher voltages, almost all components and machines must be made in an Assembler.
The Assembler is your first step toward automated, scaled production. The sooner you learn it, the faster your factory can run efficiently.
Research Road
Make research gear
The Assembler does not contain every recipe. Besides voltage tier limits, many recipes require researching the related technology first.
Press ~ to open the Technology Screen and view all technologies. Each technology marks a new stage, such as alloy smelting, which unlocks the Alloy Smelter recipe.
To research technology, you need two things:
Research Bench, for required materials
Research Equipment, consumed during research

Research Equipment for each voltage tier needs different materials, so keep upgrading your research ability as tech advances. Research unlocks recipes and new features so your factory can keep growing.
Ore Processing 2.0
Upgrade ore processing
Upgrade your ore processing system. Replace the Primitive Ore Analyzer and Primitive Ore Washer with ULV versions for better efficiency and extra resources.
The ULV Ore Analyzer can find more ore types, no longer limited to Pyrite and Chalcopyrite. To unlock new ore types, you still need to research related technologies in the Technology Screen.
For example, Cobblestone Minerals lets you extract Limonite veins from cobblestone.
The ULV Ore Washer can produce byproducts while washing target ores. These byproducts are the main source of many rare materials in Tinactory.
First Heat-Resistant Metal
Get nickel for alloys
As you explore ore processing, nickel becomes a new key material. It is needed for heat-resistant alloys such as Invar and Cupronickel, and later for high-temperature processing and advanced parts.
Nickel comes as a byproduct from limonite and banded iron ore, or by directly processing garnierite. These ores require the ULV Ore Analyzer and the limonite vein research unlock.
You will soon use it to make tougher metals that can handle heat, preparing your factory for higher voltage tiers.
Alloy Smelter
Alloy Smelter
You can now extract and process single metals, but stronger industrial structures need alloys. It is time to make your first Alloy Smelter.
After unlocking alloy smelting in the Technology Screen, craft the Alloy Smelter in the Assembler. It smelts mixed metal dusts into alloy ingots, so grind metals into dust first.
Alloys greatly expand the machines and components you can build.
Extract Sulfur
Get the key byproduct for rubber
You may have noticed sulfur from washing pyrite and chalcopyrite. Sulfur is needed for rubber, which is used in cables, machine parts, and other materials for entering LV.
In ULV, you do not need to rush rubber farming yet.
Electric Blast Furnace
Build your first multiblock machine
You are about to build your first multiblock machine: the Electric Blast Furnace. It makes steel, a core LV material, and starts mechanized smelting.
The Electric Blast Furnace is not one block. It is a structure made from several parts. Prepare these materials:
1 EBF controller
16 Heatproof Machine Casings
16 Cupronickel Furnace Coils
1 ULV Multiblock Interface
The multiblock must be placed in the required shape. After building it, connect the Multiblock Interface to power, then right-click the interface to open the machine GUI.
Tip: Tinactory includes litematica schematics for all multiblock machines. Load one to see where each block belongs.
Heat-Resistant Alloys
Make Invar and Cupronickel for hot machines
To make higher-tier industrial equipment, you need two heat-resistant alloys: Invar and Cupronickel. Both are basic materials for hot processing, especially the Electric Blast Furnace.
Invar is smelted from iron dust and nickel dust in a set ratio in the Alloy Smelter. Its heat resistance makes it useful for the Electric Blast Furnace casing.
Cupronickel is made from copper dust and nickel dust. It conducts heat and is used for Cupronickel Furnace Coils.
Hands Free
Use Basic Logistic Worker
As your machine count grows, moving items and fluids by hand becomes inefficient. Use a Logistic Worker to create your first automatic logistics network.
The Logistic Worker is a key Tinactory logistics machine with three tiers. You can now craft the Basic Logistic Worker. It moves items or fluids between machines for early automation.
Power the Logistic Worker, then right-click it to open the GUI. It has three areas:
Left: configured task list
Middle: all machines the workstation can access
Right: all ports on the selected machine
A Logistic Worker can configure multiple tasks and cycles through them. To configure a task:
Pick a task slot, then click source or target config.
Choose the machine in the middle panel.
Choose an input or output port in the right panel.
Optional: click the middle task slot to set filters for specific items or fluids.
Click the middle button to enable it.
Higher-tier Logistic Workers can run more tasks and work faster. From now on, let machines handle the carrying.
Voltage, power, and grid basics
In Tinactory, power is the core of an automated factory. Learning the basics helps you build and expand stable industrial networks.
Basic units:
EU: energy unit
EU/t: power unit, where t is one game tick
V: voltage unit
Each machine and recipe has a voltage requirement. ULV is 8V. Adjacent tiers differ by 4x, so LV is 32V. A machine's voltage must meet the recipe voltage or it cannot run.
Cable links:
Each voltage has matching cables.
Machines only connect to same-voltage cables.
Different cable voltages cannot connect. Use a transformer to bridge tiers.
Some machines like Steam Boilers have no voltage limit and can use any cable.
Cables lose power while transmitting it. At the same power, higher voltage means lower loss, so raising voltage reduces waste.

If you know GregTech, Tinactory's grid is smarter: all generators and consumers on one grid are calculated together. Machines do not request power or store buffers. If power is short, machines slow down instead of tripping.
This lets you focus on machine layout and power planning instead of complex energy routing.
Power Grid
quests.tinactory.quests.00_ultra_low_voltage.02: View machine ports
In Tinactory, ports are the key unit for machine logistics. Whether a Logistic Worker moves items or you add fluids by hand, transfers go through ports.
A port is a logical group of machine slots. Each port may be an input or output.
Use the machine GUI to view all ports. Click the port button in the lower right to open the port panel. Hover a port name, and the matching slots highlight on the right.
Understanding ports lets you configure logistics tasks more accurately and connect each task to the correct input or output.
What ports does it have?
Set recipes so machines work as intended
You have automated machines such as the Assembler, but they will not work until configured correctly.
Each machine has a button in the lower left. Click it to open the supported recipe book. Browse available recipes there and choose a target recipe.
After you select a recipe, the machine only runs that recipe and only accepts the materials it needs.
Choosing and managing recipes is essential for stable automated production.
Tip: hover a recipe book button and press R to open JEI for the recipe details.
Why is my Assembler not working?
Technology Screen
Research unlocks recipes and drives industrial progress. Learn the Technology Screen to plan your technology path efficiently.
Open the Technology Screen with the ~ key. You will see these areas:
Upper left: your current research
Lower left: all technologies
Right panel: selected technology description, prerequisites and progress
Tech status uses three colors:
Yellow: can be researched now
Red: locked; finish prerequisites first
Green: research completed
Tip: hover a technology button and press R to view in JEI which recipes it unlocks and what materials the research needs.
Research
Input and output fluids
Besides items and power, many machines handle fluids. Learning fluid input and output helps you run machines such as Ore Washers and boilers that need water or steam.
In the machine GUI, darker slots are fluid slots, not item slots. They are used to input or output liquids.
Click a fluid slot with a water or lava bucket/container to insert or extract fluid.
For other fluids, craft Fluid Cells to move and buffer them.
Add Water to Machines?
A Shiny New Friend
Extract tin and prepare for later
Before building the Electric Blast Furnace, you need tin. Even before LV, tin is required because the furnace materials need bronze, and bronze needs tin.

To get tin, research and unlock the cassiterite vein. Afterward, tin ore and cassiterite appear in your cobblestone processing system. Process them like iron and copper: wash, grind, and smelt into tin ingots.
For now tin mainly makes bronze, but in LV, tin cables become essential and your factory will consume a lot of tin.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.title: Industry Awakens
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.subtitle: Start with steel and electrical parts, build LV machines and research, then prepare for integrated circuits and MV
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.000.desc: You have raw rubber dust. Put it with sulfur dust in an alloy furnace so heat can turn it into usable rubber.
The alloy furnace will vulcanize the mix into rubber sheets, a core LV material for cables, machine parts, and later electrical builds.
If sulfur is short, check mineral byproducts from earlier processing. LV needs more and more sulfur, so secure a supply early.
After this, you have the rubber chain and your factory truly enters the electric LV age.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.001.desc: LV will quickly raise your rubber demand. Plant the rubber saplings from your starting chest and begin this key supply chain.
Plant them like normal trees, then harvest the wood. Rubber trees drop sticky resin, the base for wires, machine parts, and automation.
Grind sticky resin into raw rubber dust, the first step toward rubber. Without rubber, LV progress will stall, so keep these trees going.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.002.subt: Make casing, LV cable, and a circuit
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.002.desc: You can now craft real LV base components. These parts form the core of almost every machine in this tier.
To finish them, use nearly everything from earlier quests.
Use steel from the electric blast furnace for the LV machine casing. It is the sturdy frame for future LV machines.
Cables need tin wire and rubber insulation. Together they become the backbone of your power network.
The electronic circuit is your first LV circuit. It uses a sticky resin board, ULV vacuum tubes, resistors, and red alloy.
From here, the door to LV is open.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.003.desc: Your first electric blast furnace is the real ticket into LV. In this stage, its main job is simple: make steel.
The process is straightforward. Put iron dust in the electric blast furnace, wait for it to heat, and collect steel ingots.
Simple does not mean easy. Steel smelting is slow and uses a lot of power. Later furnace upgrades will help.
The electric blast furnace can drain your grid fast. If power cannot keep up, steel output will collapse.
Your best plan is to keep it running steadily.
Expand steam, improve generation stability, and prepare for better energy. Stable steel makes every LV machine easier.
When your first steel batch is done, your factory has entered the steel age. Make this furnace a production core.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.004.desc: Redstone is another major factory resource. This naturally powered red mineral carries signals and is vital for logic, circuits, and smarter machines.
Research redstone veins like you did for tin and coal. Then process the new minerals through washing, grinding, and handling to get redstone dust.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.005.desc: Now that you have redstone, put copper and redstone into the alloy furnace to make bright, useful red alloy.
Red alloy has two clear strengths:
It is soft and easy to work, so it cuts, presses, and forms into small parts well.
It carries electrical signals very well, making it perfect for circuits.
Because of this, it is excellent for LV circuit boards. Keep the alloy furnace powered and feed it the right copper and redstone mix.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.006.desc: In LV, coal becomes a key resource. Research coal veins, and coal minerals will start appearing in your cobblestone processing loop.
Coal is not just furnace fuel. Coal dust makes resistors for LV circuit boards, and coal is a solid boiler fuel that frees you from manual tree farming.
As your factory grows, you will want more coal. Unlock coal veins early and add them to your resource cycle.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.007.subt: Make an LV transformer to connect ULV and LV grids
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.007.desc: Good news: you can build LV machines now.
Bad news: you cannot power them yet.
LV machines will not run on plain ULV power, but your grid is still mostly ULV. The answer is the LV transformer.
An LV transformer bridges ULV and LV grids so early ULV power can drive LV equipment. LV generators need LV machines first, so this bridge is required.
You must research battery technology to unlock its recipe. You cannot make batteries yet, so this is a tech you research before using fully.
For early LV, the transformer is your temporary power solution. Without it, progress stops.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.008.subt: Make a magnetizer and magnetic steel rods
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.008.desc: LV machines need specialized drive modules, not just iron and power. Your first goal is a magnetic steel rod.
This rod is the core part of your first electric motor. Motors will also be used for electric pistons, pumps, and many later components.
Research electric motor technology, build an LV two-stage magnetizer, and process steel rods into magnetic steel rods.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.009.desc: You have magnetic steel rods. Turn one into an electric motor, the common LV drive part your factory will use constantly.
The recipe needs a magnetic steel rod, a normal steel rod, copper wire, and LV cable.
You will need motors for many machines and line upgrades. Assemble the parts and get the factory moving.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.010.desc: If vanilla furnaces still handle your smelting, upgrade to an LV electric furnace.
Research electric heating to unlock it. It runs twice as fast and no longer needs fuel.
It also uses a fair amount of power. With a weak early LV grid, it may start and then black out your line.
Build stable LV power first through better steam or generation flow. Otherwise this furnace may just sit idle.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.011.subt: Make an LV alloy furnace for advanced alloys
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.011.desc: You have used the alloy furnace. Now consider the LV alloy furnace, because some LV alloys cannot be made in the ULV version.
It uses more power than the electric furnace, and you may not need those alloys yet.
This is not urgent equipment, but it is important infrastructure. Research it, gather parts, and build it when advanced alloy recipes appear.
Wait until your LV power system can support high-draw machines running for long periods.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.012.desc: The thermal centrifuge heats and spins minerals to extract special byproducts that other processing methods cannot get.
Use it at the last step of mineral processing to squeeze out extra value, especially from rare minerals.
This stronger processing has a cost: it is currently your most power-hungry single-block machine.
Plan a separate mineral line for it so high-value ores can be handled cleanly.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.013.desc: With motors ready, craft two LV components: the electric piston and electric pump. Both use motors, mechanisms, and cables.
Electric pistons push and form the frame of many metalworking machines. Electric pumps handle fluid transfer and mixing.
You will need both often for new machines and automation. Start making these motion parts now.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.014.desc: Sensors and emitters are not consumed in bulk, but they unlock important parts of the tech tree.
LV sensors and emitters need brass, so the zinc from thermal centrifuging is useful now. They also need some circuit boards.
They unlock LV research gear, upgrade the ULV ore analyzer to LV, and later become core parts for integrated circuit production machines.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.015.desc: If your cobblestone generator now produces deepslate, upgrade your analysis tool with an LV ore analyzer.
This upgraded ULV ore analyzer can identify new vein data hidden in deepslate.
To craft it, you need the LV sensor you made earlier.
To get deepslate veins, unlock LV research and complete the needed technologies.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.016.desc: Upgrade your primitive or ULV cobblestone generator into the LV version.
The LV cobblestone generator adds deepslate. It looks like a dark stone block, but it carries a whole new resource system.
After unlocking LV research equipment, study deepslate samples to unlock new ores and processing routes.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.017.subt: Research heat and make an LV extractor
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.017.desc: Your factory can handle powders, solids, and fluids. Research heat treatment and build an LV extractor to add melting and forming workflows.
The extractor melts materials into fluids. In LV, its most important use is rubber processing.
Molten rubber can be injected into molds for cable insulation. It saves material and is faster and cleaner for automation lines.
Molten materials can later go to a fluid solidifier for shaped parts. You do not need one now, but in MV it can process metals too.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.018.desc: In LV, logistics move past hoppers. Make a conveyor belt now, since some production machines require it.
This factory-like component uses two electric motors and molten rubber. Solid rubber cannot replace it.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.019.desc: The robot arm is one of your most complex and costly components, and it is key to higher machines.
One robot arm needs 2 electric motors, 1 electric piston, and 1 LV circuit. It lets you upgrade the ULV assembler into an LV assembler.
Do not fear the cost. The robot arm unlocks more machine and component recipes and starts true automated production.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.020.desc: Your electric blast furnace still runs as ULV. With LV parts like pumps and conveyors, replace its interface to bring it into LV.
The catch is power: LV multiblocks use 4 times the original power. An LV electric blast furnace can crush a weak grid.
Make sure your power system is strong.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.021.desc: LV batteries need cadmium. It has no normal vein here; get it as a byproduct from thermal centrifuging pyrite ore.
Yield is low, but demand is also small. Process some pyrite early to prepare for batteries.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.022.subt: Make LV batteries and a battery box
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.022.desc: Batteries are finally here. In Tinactory, they store power and help stabilize your grid.
Build a battery box, connect it to the grid, and insert LV batteries. It will charge or discharge automatically with no setup.
Batteries need the cadmium you prepared, plus battery alloy and LV cable.
Your grid now has a buffer and is ready for bigger automation.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.023.subt: Craft a LV Centrifuge to separate more ore byproducts
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.023.desc: As your factory grows, you may notice the old ore processing line is stable, but its byproducts are too limited. Time for finer separation: the LV Centrifuge.
This machine spins mixed materials apart. Its most direct use now is processing ore dusts for byproducts washing cannot provide.
Its early uses are limited, but it becomes more important as research advances. Many rare materials depend on precise centrifuging.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.024.desc: Battery Alloy is made for batteries, with good insulation and sealing. Mix lead and zinc in a mixer or alloy furnace to make it.
Get lead and zinc dust from Pyrite or Chalcopyrite byproducts, or wait for sulfur veins later for steadier sources.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.025.desc: You are well into LV. To advance, reach more resources, and prepare for MV, you must start LV research.
First craft a new research platform: the LV Research Bench. It replaces your ULV bench for more advanced projects, and its recipe uses the sensor and emitter you already made.
Next, make research equipment modules in the LV Assembler. These are much more complex than ULV ones: they need LV electric motors and steel gears. Research is about to consume many parts.
Your key research goal now is unlocking deepslate veins, such as gold, sphalerite, aluminum, and diamond. You need these resources to prepare for MV.
Tip: higher tier research equipment can replace LV versions, but it is very expensive. Check voltage requirements in JEI before spending materials.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.026.desc: Your next target is zinc. You need it for brass, which leads to LV research.

You cannot get zinc ore directly yet. Sphalerite needs LV research, so for now get zinc indirectly by thermally centrifuging Chalcopyrite ore. It is inefficient, but once LV research is online you can research Sphalerite and get zinc in bulk.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.027.subt: Get aluminum dust, the star of MV
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.027.desc: Aluminum belongs to MV, but LV lets you glimpse it early. Processing Bauxite can rarely yield aluminum dust for key alloys like tough Cobalt Brass or heat-resistant Kanthal for better Electric Blast Furnace coils.

If you already have enough aluminum dust, you can try smelting aluminum ingots early and assembling some MV machines. Without late LV parts this is hard, so choose whether to rush MV or finish this stage steadily.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.028.subt: Make Cobalt Brass for the LV Cutting Machine
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.028.desc: Cobalt Brass is not cheap. Mix brass, cobalt dust, and aluminum dust in a mixer, then smelt it in a normal furnace. It needs no high heat, which is one of its few friendly traits. This strong alloy is ideal for cutting and unlocks the LV Cutting Machine.
Cobalt Brass also works as a tool material. Compared with pure cobalt, it has even better durability. Expensive, but worth it.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.029.desc: Chalcopyrite ore shows its value again: a Thermal Centrifuge can now process it into a mysterious byproduct, Cobaltite dust.

It may look plain, but it is the basis of future semiconductor tech. Further centrifuging Cobaltite gives arsenic and cobalt. Cobalt is a durable tool material, while arsenic matters later for integrated circuits.
No new vein is needed. Use your Chalcopyrite stock to prepare for the next tech jump. Watch Cobaltite output; it may not appear every time.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.030.desc: You may have found a few rubies in redstone veins. Now a second gem hides in deep graphite veins. Diamond is much rarer than ruby. For now, hammer diamond ore directly for the finished gem.
Diamonds are not just pretty; they are valuable hard industrial materials. You only need two now: one for a lathe and one for a macerator. Put them to work, not on display.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.031.subt: Make Kanthal coils; upgrade your EBF
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.031.desc: Cupronickel coils were only the start. Mix iron, aluminum, and chrome in the right ratio, then smelt Kanthal in the Electric Blast Furnace. Chrome currently comes only from ruby byproducts and is very rare. This also needs a LV Electric Blast Furnace interface and huge power. You need 16 matching coil blocks.
Research Kanthal technology before crafting the coils. You also need a little silver as base material; once you have sulfur veins, silver is manageable.
After installation, the new coils raise furnace temperature, unlock recipes, and save energy on ordinary materials. You can judge the value.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.032.subt: Research cutting; build Lathe, Cutter, Macerator
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.032.desc: You could research metal cutting at the start of LV, but materials blocked real use. Now take the step: make a saw from Cobalt Brass for the Cutting Machine, and use two diamonds for the Lathe and Macerator.
These three machines matter a lot.
The Lathe makes rods and screws efficiently, replacing slow file work and saving half the material.
The Macerator replaces mill and hammer, automating ore processing.
The Cutting Machine may first look less useful than the Lathe, mostly cutting planks and rods, but chip production will reveal its importance.
These machines are more than an efficiency boost. They mark a process leap: most metal work in your factory can now be mechanized.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.033.desc: Silicon is the base of semiconductors, but it needs doping to conduct and control signals well. The needed additives are arsenic and gallium. Extract arsenic from Cobaltite dust, and get gallium from three deepslate ores in sulfur veins.

Once you have enough of both, mix Gallium Arsenide in the mixer. It is a key semiconductor material and your first step toward integrated circuits. Process those new ores early; they are more complex than they look.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.034.desc: Ever wondered why redstone carries signals? The answer is silicon inside it. Centrifuge redstone for silicon dust, dope it with a little Gallium Arsenide, then slowly smelt it in the Electric Blast Furnace into precious Monocrystalline Silicon.
This process needs Kanthal coil heat, draws heavy power, and is very slow. Check your grid load before starting.
The good news: even though it is slow, each Monocrystalline Silicon gives many wafers, enough for many chips. This is your step into semiconductors.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.035.subt: Cut Monocrystalline Silicon into etchable wafers
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.035.desc: A whole Monocrystalline Silicon block is valuable, but it must be cut into thin wafers before chipmaking. Now that you have a Cutting Machine, use it.

Put the Monocrystalline Silicon into the Cutting Machine to slice it into wafers. Each wafer will carry future circuit patterns for laser etching. One smelt gives many slices, making this first precision cut very efficient.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.036.desc: If earlier multiblocks felt small, the Large Sifter redefines industrial scale. This huge machine fills a 5x5x5 space and is your largest build so far.

To build it, prepare one controller, 18 sifter casings, 63 solid steel machine casings, and one multiblock interface. Sifter casings need electric motors, rotors, and zinc filters, plus soldering alloy. The solid steel casings may nearly empty your steel stock.
Use litematica while building to place the structure correctly. This is both a construction challenge and proof that your industry can handle large automation.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.037.subt: Make a Laser Engraver and Ruby Lens
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.037.desc: The Sifter is not for sand; it finds flawless gems. Now you can enter the most precise circuit step: laser engraving.

First craft a Laser Engraver, using the emitter you made earlier for the laser. Then add a Ruby Lens. Sift flawless ruby, combine it with normal ruby into exquisite ruby, then polish it on the Lathe. The lens is not consumed, so each engraver only needs one.
A Ruby Lens can engrave basic integrated circuits. Later circuits require different lenses. Your chip line has now entered laser precision work.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.038.subt: Engrave and cut wafers into IC chips
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.038.desc: At last, real chips. Put your wafers in the Laser Engraver with a Ruby Lens to etch circuit patterns, producing integrated circuit wafers.

Do not install them yet; cut the wafers first. Process them again in the Cutting Machine to split each wafer into integrated circuit chips. These chips are the core of integrated circuit boards and the base for future advanced boards.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.039.subt: Make a Good Electronic Circuit toward MV
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.039.desc: The Good Electronic Circuit is the first MV circuit board you can craft. As an upgrade to the Electronic Circuit, it improves performance and lets you plan circuit automation. You need it for the LV Circuit Assembler, which will end manual circuit assembly.
Use it to build some MV machines early, or wait until integrated circuit research is done before upgrading further. Many paths are open.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.040.subt: Make a LV Circuit Assembler for auto circuits
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.040.desc: You are about to assemble your first Circuit Assembler, a LV one. It uses an MV board, but only automates LV circuit production. It sounds costly because it is an investment. Besides a Good Electronic Circuit, it needs the robot arm, conveyor, emitter, and other expensive parts you worked hard to make.
This is required for progress. Higher tier circuits will need higher voltage Circuit Assemblers. Pay the cost now, and it will repay you later.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.041.subt: Make an Integrated Logic Circuit; enter chip era
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.041.desc: Everything you have done, from smelting Monocrystalline Silicon to etching wafers and cutting chips, leads here: your first Integrated Logic Circuit.
Solder two Electronic Circuit boards together, then add Red Alloy and silver wires for the base.
Insert the IC chip you etched yourself.
Finally solder on resistors, diodes, copper wires, and tin bolts to connect everything.

This board is still LV and may not seem cheaper than Electronic Circuits while vacuum tubes are plentiful and silicon is slow. Its real value is upgrade potential. Circuit boards nest into higher tiers, but Electronic Circuits only reach MV. Integrated circuits start at LV and can be combined up to HV. Small, efficient boards are the future, and this is your entry point.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.042.desc: Parts alone are not enough in industry; solder holds them together. Antimony is the key additive in Soldering Alloy. Find it in sulfur vein byproducts. Yields are low, but you only need a little.

With enough tin and lead, add a little antimony in the alloy furnace to make stable Soldering Alloy. It joins metal parts and machine casings, and circuit production will consume a lot of it. Start stockpiling now.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.043.desc: Still hammering metal plates by hand? Build a LV Bending Machine and leave hand-made plates behind.
Metal plates are basic materials used in almost every component. The more you use, the more resources matter. Hammer crafting is slow and wastes half the material each time.
This machine is cheap and efficient, needing only an electric piston as its key part. Once it is online, switch all plate production to machines to save time and resources.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.044.desc: You have made cables for a long time, from early copper wire to hand-cut tin wire and LV cables. It is time to retire the wire cutters.
Now build a tool that truly saves material: the LV Wiremill. Its main advantage is high efficiency with no material waste.
It needs LV power, but compared with the materials it saves, the power cost is tiny.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.045.desc: You may be used to making alloys in an alloy furnace, but the LV Mixer opens a new process route.

The LV Mixer is flexible. It can combine two or more metal dusts into alloy dust, then a furnace or blast furnace can smelt it into ingots. This works for more recipes than an alloy furnace, and some high-heat metals require it.
It can also mix fluids, not just solids. Later, many processes will require mixing two liquids.
Tip: this machine does not run automatically. Start it with the recipe book.
quests.tinactory.quests.01_low_voltage.046.desc: Your machines now run on the LV grid and work well, but what if you want them faster?
Welcome to overclocking. When a machine receives voltage above its default design, it overclocks automatically: work speed doubles, and power use becomes 4x.
This trades energy for speed. Efficiency drops, but speed-first production steps can benefit from it. For now, with mostly LV machines, the rising power cost is not very visible.
The good news: future technologies will gradually unlock ways to improve this mechanic.
Understanding overclocking is essential before entering MV and HV industry. It helps you decide when to chase speed and when to chase efficiency.
After all, speed has a cost.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.title: Chem and Circuits
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.subti: Start MV: build a Distillation Tower and Vacuum Freezer, enter chemistry, etch CPU wafers, and make a CPU board.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.000.d: You already worked hard in LV to get your first aluminium dust. Now that MV has begun, aluminium becomes a main material. Put the dust into your LV Electric Blast Furnace to smelt it. Aluminium has a low melting point, so you do not need better coils yet, only stable power. The ingots will form the frame of your factory for a long time, from frames to core MV machine parts. Smelt extra now; you will be glad later.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.001.d: Your first real MV parts are MV machine casings and MV cables. They are the base of every MV machine; without them your factory can only think about upgrading. Casings use lighter aluminium, and cables now need stricter materials, so the tech tree is clearly asking more from you.

For MV circuit boards, you have two routes. If you followed chapter 2 normally, use Good Electronic Circuits. If you pushed the integrated circuit quest line and unlocked Good Integrated Circuits, you can use the better option. Either way, once these parts are ready, your factory can finally build MV machines.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.002.d: The Chemical Reactor is the central chemical machine of MV. Cobblestone generation and ore processing gave you the first raw chemicals; the reactor turns those inputs into useful materials. Research chemical technology first so you can handle liquids, gases, and solids in sealed reactions.

The reactor handles a huge number of recipes: basic acids, salts, organics, polymers, and alloy precursors all pass through it. After MV, chemistry becomes a constant need, and this machine is its hub. Build this alchemy pot to put your factory on the road to modern industry.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.003.t: Zap It Until the Elements Give In
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.003.d: The MV Electrolyzer starts many MV chemical chains. Research electrolysis first to learn element extraction at higher voltage. Once built, it can make chlorine and sodium hydroxide steadily from brine. You will need both often for ore refining and organic chemistry.

Some ores also need electrolysis to release target elements. Bauxite, for example, uses it to provide aluminium. The Electrolyzer gives your factory its first real ability to split basic elements, a key step from metallurgy into chemical industry.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.004.d: Want metal to take shape in one step? Research cold metalworking first. Then you can craft your first MV Forming Press. Its main strength is pressing materials directly into finished shapes without several machines or intermediate steps. One machine can cover many forms and clean up your layout.

The MV Forming Press needs higher voltage and more power than ordinary processing. If your grid is still weak, it may strain your supply. Check generation before running it. With stable power, it becomes one of the most reliable metal shaping machines of MV.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.005.d: The MV Cobblestone Generator is more than a simple LV upgrade. It changes your resource base. MV chemistry expands quickly, and seawater and air are two basic inputs that are easy to overlook.

The MV Cobblestone Generator lets you produce seawater and air from the void as steadily as cobblestone. Higher speed is useful, but the real value is that your chemical system stops choking on basic inputs. Electrolysis, acids, gas reactions, and larger chains will all need them. Set it up now so your MV factory does not stall later.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.006.d: Stainless steel means HV is getting close. To make this strong, corrosion resistant metal, mix iron dust with three old friends: nickel, chromium, and manganese. Nickel and chromium are familiar; manganese comes as an ilmenite byproduct.

Mix the four elements in an MV Mixer to get even stainless steel dust. Then upgrade your Electric Blast Furnace to MV so it can smelt the dust into stainless steel ingots. Its melting point is not too high, and Kanthal coils can save some power.

Stainless steel belongs mostly to HV, but you need it now for one major chemical structure: the Distillation Tower. This huge multiblock is an MV chemistry milestone and the key to oil processing. Make the stainless steel now; the big build is next.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.007.d: The MV Mixer is a full upgrade over the LV Mixer. It is faster, supports more recipes, and handles a wider range of materials. New alloys in MV need more complex preparation, and mixing is often required. Build it to smooth out both metal and chemical production.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.008.d: The MV Assembler is faster and more precise than the LV version, and will start replacing your early LV Assembler. MV adds many component recipes, and many new parts require a higher tier Assembler. This is a required upgrade for smooth MV production.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.009.d: After mass producing integrated circuits, you can tackle the second chip type: memory chips. Better circuit boards need many of them, and the process is stricter. First make a sapphire lens. It is like the ruby lens: get flawless sapphire from the Sifter, assemble it with normal sapphire and sapphire dust into exquisite sapphire, then polish it on the Lathe. The lens is not consumed during etching, so one is enough.

With the sapphire lens, use the Laser Engraver to etch memory wafers, then cut them into memory chips. The success rate is lower than for integrated circuits. This is not a bug: memory is more sensitive to contamination. Later, a cleanroom will reduce these losses. For now, make your first memory chips and push your circuits forward.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.010.d: Electrum is a good alloy of gold and silver. It has warm color and good conductivity, making it key for circuit boards and electrical parts.

Mix gold dust and silver dust, then bake the blend in an Electric Furnace, or melt both dusts together in an Alloy Smelter. Electrum becomes more important as voltage rises, and later electrical parts will use it often.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.011.d: The Advanced Integrated Circuit is your first HV circuit board and the required part for upgrading the Circuit Assembler to MV. Each Circuit Assembler tier needs the next circuit board tier, so this one is unavoidable.

Its recipe is integrated circuit nesting: use two Good Integrated Circuits as the body, then add integrated circuit chips, memory chips, and transistors for logic and storage. Electrum connects the modules. Together, these parts form your first smart core for HV.

You may wonder why you need this so early in MV. It is required for the next two major MV structures: the Distillation Tower and the Vacuum Freezer. Make it to unlock the next stage of chemical industry.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.012.d: The Distillation Tower is a true MV chemical multiblock. It splits mixed fluids back into components: salts from seawater, fractions from oil, and different gases from liquid air. The taller the tower, the more complete the separation.

It is expensive. The shell needs many clean stainless steel casings, and the controller uses several HV components and an HV circuit board, making it your costliest multiblock so far. It can be 3 to 8 layers tall. More layers give more outputs: 3 layers gives 1 product, while 8 layers can separate all 6 fractions.
This is the first real threshold for large scale chemical industry. Once the Distillation Tower runs, your factory can analyze mixed fluids and prepare for deeper modern industry.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.013.d: The Vacuum Freezer removes heat quickly under low pressure, cooling fluids until they liquefy or even solidify. Its controller needs an HV circuit board, but the rest uses MV parts and aluminium casings, so it is cheaper than the Distillation Tower. Your first use is liquefying air, then sending it to the Distillation Tower for important gases.

It is not only for air. Later, difficult metals must be melted at high temperature and then quenched quickly to gain special properties, and the Vacuum Freezer handles that process. Build it now so your factory can freeze materials on demand.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.014.s: Get rutile from bauxite or ilmenite
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.014.d: The MV Electrolyzer gives you steady aluminium and also produces a rare byproduct from bauxite: rutile. Its main content is titanium dioxide. You cannot process it much yet, but it becomes valuable for advanced materials later.
If you do not have much bauxite, use another route: smelt ilmenite with carbon dust in the Blast Furnace. It gives iron and steady rutile. Stockpile it now; you will want it later.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.015.d: Chlorine is an important inorganic chemical. You can make it steadily by electrolyzing brine or seawater. It looks hazardous, but it is extremely useful. Even in organic chemistry, chlorine appears as a key intermediate in many chains. Polymer synthesis and organic conversions will use it often. It can also make hydrogen chloride, an important acid.
Start the Electrolyzer now. Future chemistry runs better with chlorine available.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.016.d: Sodium chloride is an important inorganic input, and seawater distillation provides it easily. It also produces potassium chloride, magnesium chloride, calcium chloride, and lithium brine. You can get potassium, magnesium, and calcium elsewhere, such as calcium carbonate from stone dust and magnesium or potassium from olivine byproducts. Lithium brine has little use in MV, but in HV it becomes battery material, so save some.

Sodium chloride is used widely, but its key role is being dissolved into brine for electrolysis. Seawater can be electrolyzed directly, but distilled sodium chloride is purer, faster, and avoids wasting other seawater resources. Stable chlorine and sodium hydroxide are the base of inorganic chemistry, and sodium chloride starts it.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.017.t: Put the Sky in a Freezer
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.017.d: Liquid air is your first step in gas processing. Send air into the Vacuum Freezer, and low pressure cooling will turn it into this cold condensate. If the Vacuum Freezer is ready, connect air from your Cobblestone Generator and watch it condense quickly.
Freezing air lets you separate its components by boiling point, giving the Distillation Tower another important job.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.018.d: Send liquid air into the Distillation Tower to separate its three main components: nitrogen, oxygen, and rare argon. Nitrogen is the one you need next. Its value is not reactivity, but doing almost nothing. It is an excellent protective gas that prevents metals and other materials from oxidizing at high temperature. Use the tower for air separation and prepare for high temperature material processing.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.019.d: After unlocking oil veins, you will find three special ores in deepslate: natural gas ore, light crude oil ore, and heavy crude oil ore. Unlike normal ores, they do not need crushing, washing, or complex processing. Put them in a Centrifuge to separate the fluids directly. Natural gas is a good fuel, while light and heavy crude oil are core refinery inputs. These dark resources become key chemical stock.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.020.d: Send natural gas, light crude oil, and heavy crude oil into the Distillation Tower to get four fractions: refinery gas, naphtha, light fuel, and heavy fuel. Heavier crude gives more heavy fractions, so do not process one oil. Mix the three as needed.

These fractions are sulfur rich. Using them directly will pollute your chemical system and affect later reactions. Next comes desulfurization, which turns rough oil products into clean inputs for deeper refining.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.021.d: Desulfurization is a key refining step. React hydrogen with four oil fractions to remove sulfur impurities. The cleaned products are more stable and easier to process before deeper distillation or cracking. Hydrogen is nearly the universal reducing agent here, which is why you needed it early.
After desulfurization, some oil fractions can be distilled into organic materials. For more and better organics, you need the top end of refining: cracking.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.022.d: Biomass is a natural organic feedstock. Put crops, leaves, or seeds into an Extractor to get this pale green plant essence. With an automated farm and a steady bone meal line, you can mass-produce it. Yields differ by crop; sugary crops such as sugar cane are especially good.

Biomass is an organic chemistry route beside oil. It can be processed into ethanol and methane, replacing some oil products. It is cleaner and greener, but the core organic material you will use later, propylene, still comes only from oil. Even so, biomass lowers your oil dependence.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.023.d: Ethanol is one path from Biomass into organic chemistry. Send Biomass and oxygen into a Chemical Reactor to ferment it. Ethanol can also burn directly in a Combustion Engine as cleaner green energy, reducing your need for coal for a while.

In MV, ethanol is most useful as a route to ethylene. Dehydrate it with sulfuric acid to get this key organic monomer without refining oil. If you want chemistry without heavy oil use, ethanol is a strong green feedstock.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.024.d: Sulfuric Acid is one of the most important acids in chemistry. You will use it for ore dissolution, circuit board etching, organic dehydration, and even MV batteries. Most recipes use diluted acid, because the pure fluid is harsh enough to damage the material too.

Before the Large Chemical Reactor, your only acid route is oxidizing Hydrogen Sulfide. If you are refining oil, desulfurization supplies plenty of it. If not, synthesize a little from hydrogen and sulfur dust.

Sulfide ore processing produces lots of sulfur dioxide, but it cannot make Sulfuric Acid yet. That route needs a Large Chemical Reactor, unlocked in HV. For now, use Hydrogen Sulfide for your first bucket of Sulfuric Acid.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.025.s: Build an Automatic Farm for crops
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.025.d: If your factory needs a little green among the steel and chemistry, build an Automatic Farm. Feed it seeds and the multiblock handles tilling, planting, and harvesting, producing crops without manual hoe work.

Unlike manual farming, the Automatic Farm constantly consumes fertilizer. Use Biomass, bone meal, or proper fertilizer to keep it efficient. It was craftable in LV, but MV chemistry finally makes fertilizer production practical.
This machine automates crops and supplies stable feedstock for Biomass, organic chemistry, and green energy.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.026.d: Ethylene is one of the most critical base materials in your current organic chain. Many polymers, especially the common Polyethylene, start from ethylene. It is often the product you plan refining around.

There are two main routes. A refining setup can provide ethylene from distillation or cracking products. The Biomass route can also dehydrate ethanol with sulfuric acid, starting organic chemistry without oil.
Either way, ethylene is the key to plastics and more complex organic materials. Keep its supply stable before calling your MV chemical plant truly online.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.027.d: Polyethylene is one of the most important and common materials after MV. Machine casings, components, circuit boards, molten plastic, and plastic sheets will all keep it in your storage.

Making it is simple: put ethylene in a reaction environment with oxygen as an initiator. The ethylene molecules link into long chains and form Polyethylene. This first polymer marks the shift from raw chemistry to material manufacturing.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.028.d: Hydrogen is an important reducing agent in chemistry and is used often for MV oil processing. It has several sources, so choose what fits your factory:
The most common stable route is hydrogen byproduct from electrolyzing water or brine, a classic chlor-alkali byproduct. If your Electrolyzer runs well, that hydrogen will last a while.
Bulk hydrogen production relies on methane and organic chemistry. Methane is not easy before oil refining, but once stable, it is excellent for mass hydrogen.
There is also a niche inorganic route: mix iron dust with certain acidic fluids and you get the familiar bubbling reaction. Yes, that makes hydrogen too.
Whichever route you pick, hydrogen is central to chemistry. From oil cracking to mineral processing, keep a stable hydrogen line for advanced MV manufacturing.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.029.s: Get Methane from oil or Biomass
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.029.d: Methane is a basic organic feedstock. It is not as central as ethylene, but appears in many reactions and is excellent for large hydrogen production. With enough methane, hydrogen becomes very reliable. It can also burn in a Gas Turbine.
You can get methane from two routes. Oil refining produces it among light gases. Distilling Biomass also gives steady methane, keeping an organic chain running without oil.
Methane is an important gaseous foundation for hydrogen production, fuel power, and organic reactions.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.030.s: Get Oxygen from Liquid Air or electrolysis
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.030.d: Oxygen is a common gas used often in chemistry. Distill Liquid Air for a steady source, or collect it as a byproduct from electrolyzing water and oxygen-bearing minerals. It is ordinary in air, but useful in many reactions.
You need oxygen for high-temperature metallurgy, oxidizing inorganic compounds, acid preparation, and some polymer reactions.
Keep a stable oxygen line so your chemistry and material production can mature.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.031.d: Polyvinyl Chloride is the second common polymer you can make in MV. Compared with Polyethylene, its chlorine content improves corrosion resistance and insulation, making it useful for circuit boards, fluid pipes, and other components.

To make Polyvinyl Chloride, first make Vinyl Chloride. Before the Large Chemical Reactor, the MV route is ethane plus chlorine. Later, you can use the easier ethylene plus hydrogen chloride route. Finally, polymerize Vinyl Chloride with oxygen, turning it into PVC.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.032.s: Upgrade Circuit Assembler for CPU boards
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.032.d: CPU Circuit Boards are denser and more complex than any earlier board. Upgrade the Circuit Assembler to MV to assemble them. Besides an MV Emitter, the upgrade needs an HV Circuit Board as the control core. The upgraded machine handles finer soldering, wiring, and component placement for advanced electronics.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.033.s: Build a Cleanroom for chip etching
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.033.d: Remember the occasional failures when etching Memory Chips? The machine was fine; the environment was not clean enough. Tiny dust can ruin a wafer. As chips advance, cleanliness matters much more, so build a Cleanroom.

A Cleanroom can be any size, but its length and width must be odd because the controller belongs at the roof center. Build the shell from lots of Plastic Concrete, and make the entire roof from Filter Casings. For visibility, replace walls with Clean Glass and add a door, but keep it shut.
The Cleanroom consumes power to maintain cleanliness, and cleanliness directly affects laser etching success. Some advanced chips need more than 100% cleanliness, which future chapters will solve.

Memory Chips can still be made in a normal environment, but CPUs need at least a Cleanroom or the failure rate is brutal. To bring in power, replace one Plastic Concrete wall block with a Power Relay or Transformer.
Your electronics industry now enters the age of cleanliness.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.034.s: Make a Diamond Lens for CPU etching
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.034.d: The Diamond Lens is the key tool for advanced chipmaking and is used in the Laser Engraver for CPU wafers. The process is familiar: sort a flawless gem, craft an exquisite gem, then polish it on the Lathe. Diamond handles the stronger laser needed for precise CPU etching. Make one; it is not consumed.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.035.t: Give Silicon the Power to Think
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.035.s: Etch and cut CPU wafers into CPU chips
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.035.d: All your lens, Cleanroom, and etching prep leads to this step: CPU Chips. This is your most complex and precise chip so far, and the core of CPU Circuit Boards.

Etch CPU wafers in an MV Laser Engraver with the Diamond Lens installed. The lens handles the high energy density, and etching must happen inside a Cleanroom or contamination will ruin the wafer. Even there, success is not 100%; higher cleanliness means better odds.

After etching, send the CPU Wafer to a Cutting Machine. It no longer needs a Cleanroom, and you will get real CPU Chips. They become the foundation of HV Circuit Boards and advanced computing components.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.036.d: You can now make MV Sensors and MV Emitters. They are used for MV research devices and Laser Engravers, making them key upgrade parts. The MV Ore Analyzer also gets faster, though it cannot detect higher-tier veins.

These MV components use ruby as the laser material and electrum for high-conductivity connections. They cost more than LV glass and brass, but your current lines should handle them. Upgrade them to support research and circuit production.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.037.s: Upgrade the Laser Engraver to etch CPU wafers
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.037.d: CPU wafers need far more precision than earlier chips. Upgrade the Laser Engraver to MV for stronger, steadier, faster laser scanning. The key material is the MV Emitter, which provides the energy density needed to etch CPU patterns reliably.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.038.d: The Integrated Processor is your first MV CPU Circuit Board and a milestone for electronics. It starts as a strong MV component and can be upgraded three more times, up to IV, so this board line will follow you into HV and beyond.

Its core materials are the CPU Chip you just etched and the Plastic Circuit Board from MV research. Add common electronic parts such as resistors, capacitors, transistors, fine wire, and soldering alloy to assemble a real computing brain.

With the same Plastic Circuit Board and CPU Chip, you can also craft NAND Chips and Microprocessors. They serve as ULV and LV chips, letting CPU circuit boards replace Vacuum Tubes and old Integrated Circuits. They are costly now, but their value grows with better industry, chemistry, and cleanliness.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.039.s: Make MV research gear; unlock MV research
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.039.d: MV research is no longer casual. Its device cost reflects your real industrial level. Start with an MV Electric Pump, the upgraded motor component. The bigger test is the Plastic Circuit Board.

The Plastic Circuit Board checks your whole chemistry chain. You need steady Polyethylene or Polyvinyl Chloride for the base, plus Diluted Sulfuric Acid and Iron Chloride for etching traces. Plastic synthesis, acid production, and salt handling must all work.
Once MV research devices are built and MV research begins, the tech tree clearly pushes toward HV. Your factory is now a mid-sized technology center combining materials, chemistry, and electronics.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.040.d: Sodium Hydroxide is an essential base for MV chemistry. As acids dissolve advanced minerals, you still need to recover the minerals from solution. Sodium Hydroxide neutralizes the acid into harmless brine and lets refined minerals precipitate. It also helps control pH in organic chemistry.

There are two main routes. The direct route is brine electrolysis, which also gives chlorine. The other makes Sodium Carbonate first, then processes it into Sodium Hydroxide. In MV, that route is slower and more expensive than electrolysis.
As chemistry grows more complex, you will need Sodium Hydroxide often. Keeping Electrolyzers running is the easiest choice.
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.041.s: Build an Oil Cracker for hydrocracking or steam cracking
quests.tinactory.quests.02_medium_voltage.041.d: Cracking is the most complex step in refining. Build an Oil Cracker with HV Circuit Boards and HV components as its core, plus Clean Stainless Steel Machine Casings and Heating Coils for the shell. If you built a Distillation Tower, this is expensive but manageable.

There are two cracking methods. Hydrocracking converts heavy fractions into lighter ones for flexible refining. Steam cracking produces more useful organics and feeds many organic materials. Pick the route your factory needs.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.title: HV to Liftoff
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.000.sub: Assemble HV Machine Hulls and cables
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.000.des: HV is not a faster MV, but a new layer of infrastructure. Stainless steel frames, Polyethylene insulation, and CPU-grade circuits start becoming the default machine cost.
Prepare HV Machine Hulls and HV cables first. Later machines, power, and material routes will all build around them.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.001.sub: Connect to the HV grid with a transformer
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.001.des: In HV, loose wiring exposes problems faster. Machines draw more power, cable loss matters more, and the grid benefits from clear tiers from the start.
An HV transformer connects your existing power system to the new voltage tier. Build that bridge first, then feed HV machines steadily.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.002.sub: Use arcs to organize metal loops
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.002.des: The Arc Furnace turns high current directly into metallurgical heat, giving minerals, alloys, and recovery routes cleaner processing. It is not just a faster furnace, but the start of HV material cycling.
Once this machine is built, many later metal processes gain a steadier entry point. For a factory preparing for Titanium and Advanced Alloy, it is worth placing early.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.003.des: Ammonia is the first base gas that truly opens HV chemistry. Send steady nitrogen and hydrogen into a Chemical Reactor, and you get the core feedstock for Nitric Acid, Ammonium Chloride, Fertilizer, and alkali routes.

From here, chemistry is no longer just processing byproducts; it starts arranging element flows directly. Give Ammonia stable storage and transport, and later acid, salt, and fuel routes will run more smoothly.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.004.sub: Oxidize Ammonia into a strong acid route
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.004.des: Nitric Acid pushes the Ammonia route into a more aggressive chemical stage. It feeds Potassium Nitrate production, drives Toluene toward Gelled Toluene, and ultimately supports industrial TNT and explosive forming.

This route will keep consuming Ammonia, oxygen, and water, so do not stop at the one bucket the quest asks for. Later explosives, Fertilizer, and rocket precursor materials will all draw from this capacity.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.005.des: Sodium Carbonate makes brine more than an electrolysis feedstock. With Ammonia and carbon dioxide, you can move it into a fuller soda route while also making Ammonium Chloride for later Fertilizer.
