It is also a hub for carbonate chemistry: it can return to Sodium Hydroxide or feed later mineral and salt processing. Stabilize this route to rely less on one electrolysis chain.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.006.des: Ammonium Chloride is the solid intermediate where Ammonia meets Hydrogen Chloride. It is less visible than acids or bases, but it connects advanced chemistry to the agriculture route.
In this chapter, its most direct value is entering the Fertilizer recipe. Let Ammonia, Hydrogen Chloride, and Ammonium Chloride back each other up, and your chemical inventory becomes more flexible.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.007.des: Potassium Nitrate links the Nitric Acid route into potassium salts, forming the other key salt in the Fertilizer recipe. You already have the acid; now turn it into a solid feedstock that stores and mixes more easily.
Its value is not one batch of output, but a steady agricultural supply from advanced chemistry. The larger your Automatic Farm grows, the more this nitrate branch deserves automation.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.008.sub: Prepare Bone Meal and Fertilizer in bulk
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.008.des: Bone Meal is the simplest accelerator for automated farming, while Fertilizer is the formal HV chemical supply for the farm. Tie the line together with carbonates, Ammonium Chloride, and Potassium Nitrate, and crop output no longer depends on hand stockpiles.

This is not just a small farming task: steady crops feed back into Biomass, organic chemistry, and fuel routes. The bit of green inside a steel factory eventually becomes part of industrial throughput.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.009.sub: Let the HV Chemical Reactor carry complex reactions
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.009.des: From here, chemistry is no longer just a few buckets of fluid made on demand. The HV Chemical Reactor gives acid-base work, chlorination, organic synthesis, and hydrometallurgy prep enough speed and capacity for stable production.

Place it at the entrance to advanced chemistry, not as a machine trophy. Later Chrome, Titanium, explosives, and fuel routes will all pull reaction capacity from this node, and handcrafting will slow the whole factory fast.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.010.des: Chrome is no longer just a rare Kanthal coil ingredient. In HV it enters Sensors, Mixed Metal Ingots, and higher alloy routes, so demand becomes much steadier.
This wet route can turn gem byproduct Chrome into usable metal, but do not treat it as the only entry point. If you can supply Chrome reliably, the later material chain can keep moving.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.011.des: Rutile can finally move forward. Use chlorine and carbon to push titanium dioxide into Titanium Tetrachloride, turning the Titanium route from mineral stockpile into a real chemical process.
This is one of the key intermediates before Titanium smelting. Stabilize fluid production first, or later reduction, smelting, and quenching will run short.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.012.sub: Reduce hot Titanium at high temperature
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.012.des: Titanium Tetrachloride is only the entry point; the real metal still needs reduction and high-temperature processing. Hot Titanium Ingot means this route is only one cooling step from the finished material.
Do not rush to use it like a normal ingot. This kind of hot metal needs the Vacuum Freezer to stabilize fresh output into workable Titanium.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.013.des: After chlorination, reduction, and quenching, Titanium finally turns from rutile into usable metal. It will carry higher structures, Machine Hulls, and the next stage of material routes.
From here, the HV factory is no longer just scaling MV processes. It is preparing for EV-level material gates.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.014.des: SMD Resistors and SMD Transistors move circuit parts into denser manufacturing. They take less space than early parts, but will appear often in later HV circuits and precision machines.
Start planning bulk SMD production now. At this point, the bottleneck is no longer just chips, but whether every small component can reach the assembly line steadily.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.015.sub: Prepare stronger light for Nano chips
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.015.des: Nano-scale chips need steadier etching equipment with higher energy density. Upgrade the Laser Engraver to HV so wafer processing in the Cleanroom can meet the next precision tier.
This machine is the core entry point for the optional Nano branch. The right gem lenses, clean conditions, and stable power all decide whether your advanced chip line is reliable.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.016.sub: Leave routing space for Nano circuits
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.016.des: Advanced Circuit Boards press finer wires, solder points, and material layers into one board, creating the carrier for Nanoprocessors.
From here, circuit production becomes a precision line: boards, chips, SMD components, and clean processing all matter, and any weak output will slow later upgrades.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.017.sub: Etch Nano CPU Chips and NOR Chips
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.017.des: Nano CPU Chips and NOR Chips compress earlier CPU manufacturing to a finer scale. They rely on the HV Laser Engraver and Cleanroom, without adding another large electronics multiblock.
This step truly lights up the Nano route: one chip handles computation, the other logic and storage structure, giving later Nanoprocessors their core chips.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.018.des: The Nanoprocessor brings Nano chips, an Advanced Circuit Board, and SMD components together into a usable high-tier circuit. It marks HV electronics moving from "can etch chips" to "can assemble reliably."
If you plan to continue the Nano branch, automate these parts. Handcrafting the first one proves the route works; batch production is what gives it value.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.019.des: The Nanoprocessor Assembly organizes multiple Nanoprocessors into a higher-tier computing unit, and can serve as one endpoint for the EV circuit route.
This route is strong, but it is not the only answer for Rocket T1. If your factory can meet circuit demand with Workstations, leave the Nano array as a longer-term electronics upgrade.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.020.sub: A visible circuit route to Rocket T1
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.020.des: The Workstation upgrades the mature CPU circuit line into EV-tier circuit capability, making it the clearest and most controllable electronics exit for Rocket T1.
Compared with completing the full Nano branch, this is the better main path before your first launch. Stabilize CPU production, board supply, and circuit assembly so the rocket has a control core.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.021.sub: Push organic chemistry toward explosive intermediates
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.021.des: Gelled Toluene is the dangerous intermediate where Nitric Acid, Sulfuric Acid, and Toluene meet. It pulls aromatic hydrocarbons from refining into HV chemistry and makes TNT more than a block found underground.
This step is best tied into stable acid and Toluene supplies. What you really need later is repeatable industrial explosive production, not one-off blasting toys.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.022.des: TNT is not combat gear here; it is part of the forming process. Assemble Gelled Toluene and Polyethylene into stable explosives so the later Implosion Compressor has a reliable consumable.
From this point on, explosions are part of production planning. Automate TNT like any other factory material, because Advanced Alloy will consume it as a normal ingredient.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.023.des: The Implosion Compressor pushes normal pressure processing further: controlled detonation finishes dense forming in an instant.
It does not replace your regular Compressor. It handles materials that must be shaped by shock, and the later Advanced Alloy route will use this ability soon.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.024.sub: Press multiple metals into one billet
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.024.des: Mixed Metal Ingot is not a normal alloy ingot, but an intermediate billet for high-strength composite material. Chrome, Aluminium, Stainless Steel, and Soldering Alloy are organized here into a stable structure.
Its value is not standalone use, but paving the way for Advanced Alloy. Smooth out this material line, and later research equipment and rocket structures will meet less resistance.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.025.des: Advanced Alloy is the point where late HV materials start getting heavy. It combines Mixed Metal Ingot with shock forming to create composite plates that can meet higher strength requirements.

You will meet it repeatedly, from research equipment to rocket-related structures. This is not just a material task; it checks whether your Chrome route, implosion forming, and basic logistics can work together.
Put it into stable production early instead of scraping together one recipe at a time. Later large goals will turn this "small amount of expensive material" into continuous demand.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.026.sub: Research starts checking your HV industry
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.026.des: The cost of HV Research Equipment reflects factory strength more directly than the previous stage: HV logistics parts, Advanced Alloy, and stable assembly all need to be ready.
Research is no longer just consuming an item. It confirms whether you have a real HV manufacturing base; prepare it so later technology can keep moving toward the rocket goal.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.027.des: Rockets are not impressed by regular Diesel. Connect refining to the cetane-boosting route and prepare enough Cetane Boosted Diesel; only then have you really started laying Rocket Fuel logistics.
This fuel line is a continuous rocket project demand, not a last-minute bucket poured into the tank. Keep cracking, Diesel, and additive routes stable so the launch site does not stall on fuel night.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.028.sub: Bring advanced circuit assembly to HV pace
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.028.des: The HV Circuit Assembler pushes automatic circuit assembly to a higher voltage tier. More complex circuits need finer soldering, positioning, and supply rhythm, and old equipment is no longer suited to carry all the load.
With it, the electronics route after the Workstation is easier to scale. Feed SMD components, boards, and chips into automation so later large projects are not blocked by circuits.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.029.sub: Raise the launch site's foundation first
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.029.des: Launch Site Base is the first step that turns rocket engineering from a material list into a field structure. It provides the base frame for the full launch site and puts Advanced Alloy and HV research into real construction.
From here, the goal is no longer making one machine, but building infrastructure that can support a launch flow. Leave it room, and leave interfaces for later fuel and logistics.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.030.sub: Converge HV industry into a launch platform
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.030.des: Rocket Launch Site is the physical project of late HV: materials, research, electronics, logistics, and fuel routes all converge here into a facility that can serve a rocket.
Its meaning is not just "build a multiblock." From the base to the main structure, you are fixing the whole chapter's industrial capability into a spaceflight platform.
Once it is built, the remaining problem is clear: prepare Rocket T1 itself, and let this launch site wait for its first real passenger.
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.031.sub: The first launch of the HV era
quests.tinactory.quests.03_high_voltage.031.des: Rocket T1 is the endpoint of the HV chapter, and the first time your factory truly crosses the surface boundary. To reach it, you have compressed chemistry, materials, electronics, research, and fuel routes into one goal.

This is not the victory of one recipe, but the acceptance test for the whole HV factory: Advanced Alloy supports the structure, the fuel route provides thrust, EV circuits handle control, and the launch site receives the final liftoff.
Complete it, and new resources, new environments, and the next stage of industry will open. Now send the rocket off the ground and extend Tinactory's production line beyond the sky.
Turn Rocket T1 into a new resource boundary
After Rocket T1, the HV Stone Generator is no longer just producing old stone faster. Upgrade it for steady Netherrack output, then let the HV Ore Analyzer read the vein signals inside it.

This opens the EV starting resource map: quartz, topaz, lava, and tungsten all branch out from Netherrack. The rocket brought back more than distance; it brought a whole new layer of industrial feedstock.
Obtain Certus Quartz Dust and Nether Quartz Dust
Netherrack veins bring Certus Quartz and Nether Quartz into steady supply. First connect the dust route to storage; these will become the quartz base for continued EV component upgrades.
The full Digital Storage route belongs in the Logistics chapter. EV only needs the material value of Certus Quartz and Fluix for now, so later Sensors, Emitters, and components can start moving.
Build the multiblock for crystal growth
The Autoclave turns crystal work from waiting and gathering into controlled production. It manages pressure, temperature, and solution conditions, so dusts can grow back into usable crystals.
Certus Quartz and Fluix crystallization still fits within HV voltage capability, but Rocket T1 delayed the resource entry until here. Now is the right time to bring this machine into early EV.
Start a scalable crystal loop from dust
Once Certus Quartz Dust becomes seeds, the Autoclave can turn one-time vein output into a loop. Start it with dust, feed crystals back in, and Certus Quartz becomes fit for long-term consumption.
Mix it first in an HV Mixer
Fluix Dust does not need the Autoclave to start. Send Certus Quartz, Nether Quartz, and Redstone into an HV Mixer to get the mixed dust for this EV component line.
Connect mixed dust to the crystal loop
With Certus Seeds and Fluix Dust ready, Fluix can also enter the Autoclave loop. The crystals produced here serve EV components directly, without opening the full Digital Storage route yet.
Stabilize this small loop first. When later builds need Fluix as a quartz material, the factory will not fall back to small batches of manual crafting.
Pull fluorine chemistry from Netherrack gold veins
Netherrack gold veins do more than refill gold. Topaz and Blue Topaz bring fluorine into your chemical system, making the next Hydrogen Fluoride and PTFE route visible.
First make both topaz dusts part of stable processing, then connect the acid route. EV corrosion-resistant materials begin with this gem line that looks minor at first.
The Netherrack Lava Ore vein is more than a heat source. First recover Magma Blocks from the vein route, then centrifuge them into a packaged Lava supply.
A full Iron Fluid Cell proves the Lava route is connected. From here, Lava can feed Steam support, Obsidian production, and later high-temperature processing without manual bucket transfers.
With Lava available as a fluid, Obsidian becomes a controlled recipe instead of a manual placement trick.
React Lava with Water, handle the Steam output, and stockpile Obsidian for later processing routes that need the block in real quantity.
Make the Rocket T1 fluorine route flow
Hydrogen Fluoride is one of the acidic fluids early EV needs to treat seriously. Netherrack gem resources from Rocket T1 turn this route from a theoretical recipe into a sustainable chemical input.
Preparing a full fluid cell is not the endpoint; it only confirms the line is connected. PTFE will keep consuming it, and small hand batches will collapse quickly under reactor-scale demand.
Make sheets and pipes for harsher reactions
PTFE matters because it is inert. When ordinary materials fail under acids, solvents, and high-temperature processes, it can still stay where it belongs.
Sheets enter the Large Chemical Reactor structure, while pipes prepare the IV Electric Pump route. Make PTFE a stable material, not a one-off craft for this quest.
Move EV chemistry into scaled production
The Large Chemical Reactor is the early EV dividing line. The Netherrack resources, Hydrogen Fluoride, and PTFE from before all converge here into a larger reaction space and steadier throughput.
From this point on, chemistry is no longer just putting a few inputs into one reactor and waiting. Multiple inputs, multiple fluids, and heavier polymer routes start demanding real production lines.
Build it, and the later synthetic rubber, silicone rubber, epoxy, and carbon plate routes will have enough industrial footing. The real EV chemistry chapter starts with this machine.
Scale synthetic rubber into LCR production
The LCR lets Styrene and butadiene polymerize steadily, so Raw SBR is no longer a small lab intermediate.
This route gives later high-grade rubber enough capacity for EV insulation, seals, and component demand.
From PDMS to EV cable jackets
PDMS connects the silicon route to real high-tier insulation, then processes into tougher Silicone Rubber.
EV Cable needs Silicone Rubber Foil as its jacket layer. Stabilize this line so later EV base components can actually carry power.
Lock a light frame in Epoxy
Carbon Mesh is where the composite route closes: Molten Epoxy fixes loose fibers into a pressable structural layer.
Send it to the Implosion Compressor next, where shock forming turns the mesh into Carbon Plate.
Rocket T2 will ask for these plates directly. This is not a side material, but part of the next launch's structure bill.
Bring cables and hulls to the new voltage
EV Cable and EV Machine Hull turn the earlier Silicone Rubber, material processing, and circuit prep into real infrastructure.
From here, EV is not just a target voltage. It is the production base for more machines and components.
Shape IV components at EV voltage
The EV Assembler pushes assembly precision another step, and IV-tier parts now need this voltage to form.
Its first job is the IV Electric Pump for Rocket T2. Put this machine on the line so later components have a real outlet.
Open the Tungsten route from Tungstate dust
After Rocket T1, Netherrack is no longer just another stone base. It becomes the entry point for the Tungstate route, turning Tungsten from a future word into a planned resource.
This step only gets Tungstate dust. The metal still needs chemical extraction and high-temperature smelting, but the EV material gate starts here.
Tungstate cannot become usable metal directly. Use wet processing and chemical treatment to move Tungsten from mineral form into Tungsten Trioxide dust.
This is not an LCR acceptance point, but the Tungsten route's own extraction step. Keep acid, alkali, and powder loops stable so later high-temperature reduction stays fed.
Smelt Tungsten ingots in an HV blast furnace
Tungsten Trioxide reaches the metallurgy stage here. Send the reduced powder into the HV Electric Blast Furnace, and Tungsten ingots become this branch's first real metal output.
Tungsten is not valuable as warehouse stock alone. Its value is pushing steel to higher strength, and Tungsten Steel will soon carry the material pressure of IV components.
Prepare high-strength alloy for IV components
Tungsten Steel combines Tungsten heat resistance with a structural steel base, becoming the hard material demanded by IV components.
From now on, some machine parts no longer accept ordinary Titanium or Stainless Steel. Stabilize Tungsten Steel production so later motors, pumps, and component casings have a material base.
Extract Cadmium and Neodymium from Rare Earth
Rare Earth dust starts showing its value here: processing splits it into Cadmium dust and Neodymium dust, pulling earlier scattered byproducts into the late EV material system.
Cadmium is the parallel gain, but Neodymium is the immediate target. IV Electric Motor needs Magnetic Neodymium, so stabilize the Neodymium source first.
Prepare strong magnets for IV motors
Neodymium dust is only feedstock. IV Electric Motor needs Magnetic Neodymium that can provide a stable strong magnetic field. Shape the Neodymium, then let magnetizing equipment finish the job.
This gate is small, but it can stop the whole component route. Once magnet supply is stable, IV Electric Motor can truly enter assembly.
Connect oil gas to light fuel
Send ethane and propane into the mixing route to package this batch of LPG. It reuses your refining and light hydrocarbon base, with no LCR required, and prepares a better fuel side for Rocket T2.
Liquid Oxygen checks your Vacuum Freezer and cryogenic fluid handling, not an LCR. Push stable oxygen into liquid form so the Rocket T2 fuel route has a usable oxidizer.
LPG provides the fuel side, and Liquid Oxygen provides the oxidizer. When these short routes combine into Rocket Fuel, Rocket T2 propulsion no longer belongs to the old diesel era.
Converge materials into a drive core

IV Electric Motor is not just an old motor moved up one tier. Silicone Rubber, Tungsten Steel, and Magnetic Neodymium meet here, checking whether your polymer, alloy, and Rare Earth routes are stable.
The recipe also pulls in IV cable. It depends on Silicone Rubber Foil and Annealed Copper Wire, and does not need its own node, but prepare it before automated assembly.
Once this motor is made, the IV component route starts moving. Rocket T2's IV Electric Pump will push the material list further from here.
The fluid component Rocket T2 needs
With IV Electric Motor ready, the next step is installing it into an electric pump. PTFE pipes, Titanium parts, and EV Assembler capability are all checked in the recipe.
Rocket T2 needs four IV Electric Pump units, so this is not a one-off display item. Make these IV components repeatable early so rocket assembly does not stop on the final slot.
Choose CPU Mainframe or Nano Computer
This checks for any IV-tier circuit, without forcing one electronics route. At this stage, CPU Mainframe or Nano Computer can both satisfy the tag requirement.
The point is bringing circuit production up to the control tier Rocket T2 accepts. Do not push the target to Quantum Processor yet; that is outside this EV prep section.
Push EV industry into its second launch
Rocket T2 is the EV chapter's full acceptance test: post-Rocket T1 resources, LCR polymers, entry IV components, fuel routing, and logistics storage all converge here.

Carbon Plates put Epoxy and Carbon Fiber routes into the structure. IV Electric Pump and IV-tier circuits handle propulsion control and fluids, while Advanced Alloy from HV still carries aerospace stress.

Do not treat 1M ME Storage Component as side decoration. It comes from the Logistics chapter, but here it reminds you that the next stage of spaceflight already needs materials, data, and automation together.
The Cumene Process organizes benzene, propylene, and oxygen into a paired product stream: Phenol on one side, Acetone on the other.
Acetone feeds the Epoxy route, and Phenol will not sit idle either. Keeping this LCR process continuous is far safer than topping up barrels by hand.
Prepare the other half of Epoxy
Epichlorohydrin comes from another post-LCR chemical chain. It does not depend on Phenol or Acetone, but meets them at the Epoxy stage.
Reserve stable propylene, chlorine, and sodium hydroxide early, and the Epoxy route will not stall on one reagent.
Press two organic routes into structure
Epoxy brings Epichlorohydrin, Phenol, Acetone, and sodium hydroxide into one LCR recipe, making it a tougher EV polymer gate.
Molten Epoxy fits fluid handling, while Epoxy Sheet brings the material back to normal assembly lines.
Later Carbon Fiber composites consume this capacity directly. Do not stop at quest amounts; start planning continuous production here.
Carbon Ingot and Carbon Fiber come first, preparing the frame for the lightweight high-strength material route. This line does not need to wait for Epoxy.
The real composite strength comes from later binding and forming, but Carbon Fiber itself needs stable supply first.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.title: Logistics
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.000.title: Put Chests on the Grid
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.000.subtitle: Make a Basic Electric Chest
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.000.descripti: Electric Chests bring item buffering into the machine network, so every step does not have to pass through your inventory.
Start with the Basic version and leave room for inputs, outputs, and temporary buffers. Logistics is about making materials move to the right place by themselves.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.001.title: Buffer Fluids Too
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.001.subtitle: Make a Basic Electric Tank
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.001.descripti: Electric Tanks handle the fluid side of buffering. Steam, water, acids, and fuels all become easier to automate when they have stable storage.
Use the Basic tank to learn the input and output rhythm. Higher-tier tanks will keep expanding capacity and throughput.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.002.title: Item Buffer Upgrade
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.002.subtitle: Move Electric Chests to Good capacity
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.002.descripti: The Good Electric Chest is not the end of storage; it turns early lines from temporary boxes into sustainable buffers.
Once machines run in parallel, chest capacity directly affects downtime. Give important inputs and byproducts a larger buffer first.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.003.title: Fluid Buffer Upgrade
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.003.subtitle: Make a Good Electric Tank
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.003.descripti: The Good Electric Tank fits the growing fluid load of chemistry, boilers, and fuel lines.
Stable fluid buffers absorb production swings, keeping reactors, turbines, and fuel machines from stopping over one missing batch.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.004.title: Better Logistic Worker
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.004.subtitle: Move items and fluids at MV pace
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.004.descripti: The MV Logistic Worker turns automatic transport from a starter tool into a real production component. More tasks and faster cycles reduce manual pressure in multi-machine routes.
Use it on stable repeated routes: ore processing, chemical feedstock, boiler water, and product return are all good first targets.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.005.title: Faster Logistics Station
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.005.subtitle: Make an EV Logistic Worker
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.005.descripti: The EV Logistic Worker increases task capacity and execution speed again, ready for production lines that have grown beyond small batches.
By now, logistics is not just convenience; it protects throughput. Keep important lines running automatically before digital storage starts receiving them.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.006.title: Large Fluid Buffer
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.006.subtitle: Make an Advanced Electric Tank
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.006.descripti: The Advanced Electric Tank gives fuels, acids, and coolant loops a wider buffer.
When a line starts moving fluids continuously, capacity becomes reliability. Put fragile fluids into large buffers before adding heavier control.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.007.title: Large Item Buffer
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.007.subtitle: Make an Advanced Electric Chest
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.007.descripti: The Advanced Electric Chest can carry heavier intermediates, components, and byproducts after HV.
It will not replace a full ME network, but it keeps scattered lines from blocking before their materials enter digital storage.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.008.title: Connect ME Storage
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.008.subtitle: Make an ME Storage Interface
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.008.descripti: The ME Storage Interface bridges ordinary machine logistics and digital storage, so later materials do not stay scattered across chests and tanks.
Prepare the interface first. Once quartz, Fluix, and storage components are ready, this chapter can move from transport networks to unified storage.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.009.title: First 1M Component
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.009.subtitle: Make a 1M ME Storage Component
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.009.descripti: The 1M ME Storage Component is the core piece that makes digital storage real. It compresses crystals, cores, and circuit materials into scalable capacity.
Item and fluid storage cells both build around it. Make this tier repeatable before pushing capacity higher.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.010.title: Certus Quartz Entry
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.010.subtitle: Obtain Certus Quartz Crystal
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.010.descripti: Certus Quartz is the quartz base of the ME route. It comes from EV resources, but becomes part of the storage structure here.
Do not treat it as a one-off quest material. Digital storage keeps consuming crystals and dusts, so connect vein processing and crystal loops early.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.011.title: 1M Item Cell
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.011.subtitle: Make your first ME Item Storage Cell
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.011.descripti: The Item Storage Cell combines a 1M component, storage cores, and casing into usable digital inventory.
From here, many scattered items can move into one managed system. Reserving ME capacity for common materials greatly reduces chest walls and manual counting.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.012.title: Fluix Crystal
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.012.subtitle: Feed advanced storage components
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.012.descripti: Fluix Crystal is no longer only an EV component material. 16M and higher storage components consume it directly, moving ME expansion from dust cores into the crystal route.
Stabilize the EV dust and Autoclave loop first, then feed crystals into logistics so storage upgrades do not stall here.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.013.title: Paired Storage Cores
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.013.subtitle: Make Annihilation and Formation Cores
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.013.descripti: Annihilation Core and Formation Core are the two read and write ends of ME storage structure. One receives, and one outputs.
They are usually consumed together, so they belong in stable assembly. With both ready, cells, drives, and interfaces become much smoother.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.014.title: 1M Fluid Cell
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.014.subtitle: Make an ME Fluid Storage Cell
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.014.descripti: The Fluid Storage Cell brings acids, fuels, and intermediate fluids into digital storage instead of adding more tanks forever.
It is best for fluids you need long term but not always in motion. Local lines still need buffers; ME handles central stock.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.015.title: 4M Expansion
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.015.subtitle: Make a 4M ME Storage Component
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.015.descripti: The 4M component moves digital storage from the first usable capacity into a clearly expandable tier.
Capacity upgrades repeat the previous material structure. Once 1M components, cores, and quartz are stable, expansion stops being a side project.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.016.title: 16M Expansion
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.016.subtitle: Make a 16M ME Storage Component
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.016.descripti: The 16M component starts pushing the ME system toward large-scale storage for mid- and late-game material growth.
If every earlier tier was hand-crafted, the cost becomes obvious here. Automating the storage component route will pay back quickly.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.017.title: 64M Expansion
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.017.subtitle: Make a 64M ME Storage Component
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.017.descripti: The 64M component is the high-capacity endpoint of this logistics section, giving a large factory a quieter inventory base.
The larger the capacity, the more important clear input and output planning becomes. Make digital storage an observable stock center, not a hidden sink.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.018.title: Give Cells a Rack
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.018.subtitle: Make an ME Drive
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.018.descripti: The ME Drive holds storage cells, turning digital storage from loose items into a system block.
Cells need a stable place to live. Put the drive near your ME network entry so later expansion and maintenance stay easy.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.019.title: Signals Join Logistics
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.019.subtitle: Make an ME Signal Controller
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.019.descripti: The ME Signal Controller connects redstone logic to the signal network, letting external signals participate in machine control.
Use it to switch lines, limit refills, or combine with detectors for stock thresholds. The more automated logistics becomes, the more it needs reliable stop conditions.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.020.title: Redstone Still Matters
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.020.subtitle: Prepare a Redstone Torch
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.020.descripti: Digital logistics does not make basic redstone disappear. A Redstone Torch is the simple entry point for the signal-control route.
Prepare this small part, then connect it to ME control equipment. Complex systems often still begin with one stable signal.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.021.title: See Stock Changes
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.021.subtitle: Make an ME Storage Detector
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.021.descripti: The ME Storage Detector turns storage state into a usable signal. When stock is full, empty, or at a threshold, production lines can react.
Pair it with the Signal Controller to reduce overproduction and wake refill lines when important materials run low.
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.022.title: Digital Multiblock Interface
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.022.subtitle: Connect EV multiblocks to digital logistics
quests.tinactory.quests.logistics.022.descripti: The EV Digital Interface lets large machines sit close to ME and automatic logistics instead of relying on manual transfer.
This ties the chapter's chests, tanks, workers, and ME storage back into larger production equipment. Later automation will depend more and more on this interface layer.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.title: Tracing Deep Veins
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.subtitle.0: Use research to unlock ore veins and let the Ore Analyzer read secrets from stone
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.000.title: Limonite Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.000.subtitle: Research Stone Ores to unlock the Limonite Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.000.descripti: The Limonite Vein yields Limonite, Banded Iron, and Garnierite. It is the first vein unlocked by Stone Ores research.
You need ULV research and an Ore Analyzer.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.001.title: Tin Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.001.subtitle: Unlock Tin
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.002.title: Redstone Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.002.subtitle: Unlock Redstone
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.002.descripti: The Redstone Vein yields Redstone, Ruby, and Cinnabar.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.003.title: Coal Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.003.subtitle: Unlock Coal
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.004.title: Magnetite Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.004.subtitle: Research Deepslate Ores to unlock the Magnetite Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.004.descripti: The Magnetite Vein yields Magnetite and Gold. It is the first vein unlocked by Deepslate Ores research.
You need LV research and an Ore Analyzer.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.005.title: Graphite Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.005.subtitle: Unlock Graphite
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.006.title: Bauxite Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.006.subtitle: Unlock Bauxite
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.006.descripti: The Bauxite Vein yields Bauxite and Ilmenite.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.007.title: Sulfur Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.007.subtitle: Unlock Sulfide Ore
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.007.descripti: Sulfide Ore: Sphalerite, Galena, and Silver.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.008.title: Gem Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.008.subtitle: Unlock Gems
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.008.descripti: The Gem Vein yields Emerald and Sapphire.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.009.title: Oil Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.009.subtitle: Unlock Oil
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.009.descripti: Oil Vein: Natural Gas, Light Oil, Heavy Oil.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.010.title: Gold Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.010.subtitle: Research Netherrack Ores to unlock the Gold Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.010.descripti: The Gold Vein yields Gold Ore, Topaz, and Blue Topaz. It is the first vein unlocked by Netherrack Ores research.
You need HV research, an Ore Analyzer, and Tier 1 Rocket unlocked first.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.011.title: Quartz Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.011.subtitle: Unlock Quartz
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.011.descripti: Quartz Vein: Nether Quartz, Certus Quartz, Lapis Lazuli.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.012.title: Tungstate Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.012.subtitle: Unlock Tungstate
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.012.descripti: The Tungstate Vein yields Tungstate and Molybdate.
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.013.title: Pitchblende Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.013.subtitle: Unlock Pitchblende
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.014.title: Magma Vein
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.014.subtitle: Unlock Lava Ore
quests.tinactory.quests.ore_veins.014.descripti: The Lava Ore vein yields Magma Blocks.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.title: Power Generation
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.000.de: Steam Turbines are one of the first stable power entries, converting boiler steam into usable energy.
The LV version builds on ULV experience. Power is no longer just keeping machines alive; it starts becoming planned supply.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.001.de: The High Pressure Steam Boiler turns fuel and water into a much stronger steam supply, which is key to expanding steam power.
Keep fuel, water, and steam buffers stable. The larger the boiler output, the more visible every supply gap becomes.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.002.de: The Gas Turbine gives light fuels a direct path into the grid, especially byproducts from refining and gas handling.
Once LPG and other gas routes are stable, it becomes a cleaner supplement than hand-fed solid fuel.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.003.su: Prepare light fuel for Gas Turbines
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.003.de: LPG organizes light hydrocarbons from refining into a fuel that can be stored and moved.
Fill an Iron Fluid Cell first to prove the line is connected. Later, it can support gas power and continue into Rocket Fuel.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.004.de: Rocket Fuel is not only a spaceflight material; it proves you can merge fuel and oxidizer routes into a high-energy fluid.
Its place in the power chapter is a reminder that fuel industry spans both grids and rockets. Stabilize it before larger goals need propellant.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.005.de: The Combustion Generator uses more traditional liquid fuels, making it a natural outlet for Diesel and other refining products.
Its value is stability and easy expansion: if the fuel line keeps flowing, the grid has a steady generation backbone.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.006.de: Diesel is the core fuel that brings refining into power generation, better suited to continuous supply than early solid fuel.
Connect Diesel production, buffering, and generator input into one loop, and the grid gains a predictable energy route.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.007.de: Cetane Boosted Diesel strengthens regular Diesel, preparing a better fuel base for high-tier demand and later rockets.
This route consumes more chemistry and refining capacity, so automate it early. One quest cell will not keep up for long.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.008.su: Scale three generator routes into a multiblock
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.008.de: The Large Turbine gathers steam, gas, and combustion power experience into multiblock scale, greatly raising grid throughput.
It checks more than one controller: fuel, fluids, materials, and power planning all need to support continuous large energy equipment.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.009.su: Feed industrial steam to Large Turbines
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.009.de: The Large Boiler expands steam production to multiblock scale, giving Large Turbines a strong enough fluid source.
It magnifies fuel and water pressure. Plan buffers and logistics interfaces before tying the whole steam system into the main grid.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.010.de: Coolant moves power generation from simple burning into heat transport. A full Hot Coolant cell shows fluid handling is serving more complex energy machines.
This loop will carry safety and efficiency pressure in high-heat machines like Nuclear Reactors, and should not rely on manual filling.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.011.de: The Power Substation organizes large grids, connecting scattered generation and factory loads into a clearer energy structure.
As generation routes multiply, power problems are not only about total output. The network also needs better collection, distribution, and room to expand.
quests.tinactory.quests.power_generation.012.de: The Nuclear Reactor pushes generation into dense energy output while checking coolant, EV assembly, and Advanced Alloy foundations together.
Before building it, confirm the coolant loop and grid can handle continuous operation. Nuclear power brings strong output and stricter logistics planning.
